content:
# Exodus Chapter 34 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 104–106; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 77–78 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_034_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_034_page_002.jpg`, `EXO_034_page_003.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_034_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_034_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — THE NAME PROCLAIMED:** The Leningrad Codex presents Exodus 34 across pages 104–106, continuing directly from YHWH's promise to pass his goodness before Moses. This chapter is the theological summit of the Torah's portrait of YHWH: the divine name is proclaimed in the great attribute formula (vv. 6–7), new tablets replace the shattered ones, and a renewed covenant is established. The chapter closes with Moses' face radiating light — the physical aftereffect of standing in YHWH's presence. The Vaticanus text (pp. 77–78) follows MT closely but shows notable divergences in the covenant stipulations section.

---

## New Tablets and the Theophany Prepared (vv. 1–4)

**1** And YHWH said to Moses, "Carve [pesal] for yourself two tablets of stone [shnei-lukhot avanim] like the first ones [ka-rishonim], and I will write [ve-khatavti] upon the tablets the words [ha-devarim] which were upon the first tablets [ha-lukhot ha-rishonim] which you shattered [asher shibbarta]."

> **Key terms:** פְּסָל (pesal) = "carve/hew out" — Qal imperative; Moses must prepare the medium, but YHWH writes the content. שִׁבַּרְתָּ (shibbarta) = "you shattered" — Piel of שׁבר; the responsibility falls squarely on Moses. **LXX:** λάξευσον ("hew out"), matching MT.

**2** "And be prepared [ve-heyeh nakhon] for the morning, and go up [ve-alita] in the morning to Mount Sinai [el-har Sinai], and station yourself [ve-nitsavta] for me there upon the top [rosh] of the mountain."

> **Key terms:** נָכוֹן (nakhon) = "prepared/established" — from כון. וְנִצַּבְתָּ (ve-nitsavta) = "and you shall station yourself" — Niphal of נצב; the same verb used of YHWH's designated standing-point in 33:21. **LXX:** καὶ στῆθι ("and stand"), matching MT.

**3** "And no man [ve-ish] shall go up [ya'aleh] with you, and also [ve-gam] no man [ish] shall be seen [yera'eh] in all the mountain; also [gam] the flock [ha-tson] and the cattle [ve-ha-baqar] shall not graze [al yir'u] facing [el-mul] that mountain."

> **Key terms:** אַל יִרְעוּ (al yir'u) = "let them not graze" — even animals are excluded; the mountain's holiness demands total isolation. **LXX:** adds μηδὲ πρόβατα μηδὲ βόες ("neither sheep nor cattle"), closely matching MT.

**4** And he carved [va-yifsal] two tablets of stone [lukhot avanim] like the first ones [ka-rishonim]; and Moses rose early [va-yashkem] in the morning and went up [va-ya'al] to Mount Sinai [el-har Sinai] as YHWH had commanded [tsivvah] him, and he took [va-yiqqakh] in his hand the two tablets of stone.

> **Key terms:** וַיַּשְׁכֵּם (va-yashkem) = "and he rose early" — Hiphil of שׁכם; Moses' eager obedience. **LXX:** ὀρθρίσας ("having risen early"), matching MT.

---

## The Proclamation of the Name (vv. 5–9)

**5** And YHWH descended [va-yered] in the cloud [be-anan] and stationed himself [va-yityatsev] with him there, and he proclaimed [va-yiqra] the name of YHWH [be-shem YHWH].

> **Key terms:** וַיִּתְיַצֵּב (va-yityatsev) = "and he stationed himself" — Hithpael of נצב; YHWH takes his stand alongside Moses. וַיִּקְרָא בְשֵׁם יהוה (va-yiqra be-shem YHWH) = "and he proclaimed in/by the name YHWH" — the subject is ambiguous (Moses or YHWH); most interpreters read YHWH as the proclaimer, confirmed by v. 6. **LXX:** κατέβη κύριος ἐν νεφέλῃ ("the Lord descended in a cloud"), then ἐκάλεσεν τῷ ὀνόματι κυρίου ("he called by the name of the Lord").

**6** And YHWH passed [va-ya'avor] before his face [al-panav] and proclaimed [va-yiqra]: "YHWH, YHWH, a God [El] compassionate [rakhum] and gracious [ve-khannun], slow [erekh] of anger [appayim] and abundant [ve-rav] of loyal-love [khesed] and truth [ve-emet] —"

> **Key terms:** רַחוּם (rakhum) = "compassionate" — from רֶחֶם (rekhem, "womb"); visceral, maternal compassion. חַנּוּן (khannun) = "gracious/showing favor" — from the same root as חֵן (khen). אֶרֶךְ אַפַּיִם (erekh appayim) = literally "long of nostrils," i.e., slow to flare in anger. חֶסֶד (khesed) = "loyal-love/covenant-faithfulness" — the most theologically loaded term in the Hebrew Bible for YHWH's relational commitment. אֱמֶת (emet) = "truth/reliability/faithfulness." **LXX:** οἰκτίρμων καὶ ἐλεήμων, μακρόθυμος καὶ πολυέλεος καὶ ἀληθινός ("pitiful and merciful, long-suffering and abundant in mercy and true") — renders khesed as ἔλεος ("mercy").

**7** "— keeping [notser] loyal-love [khesed] for thousands [la-alafim], bearing [nose] iniquity [avon] and transgression [va-fesha] and sin [ve-khatta'ah]; and he will certainly not acquit [ve-naqqeh lo yenaqqeh] — visiting [poqed] the iniquity [avon] of fathers [avot] upon sons [al-banim] and upon sons of sons [al-bene vanim], upon the third [al-shileshim] and upon the fourth generation [ve-al-ribbe'im]."

> **Key terms:** נֹצֵר (notser) = "guarding/keeping" — Qal participle of נצר; YHWH actively preserves his khesed. נֹשֵׂא (nose) = "bearing/lifting/carrying away" — Qal participle of נשׂא; the same verb used for bearing a burden. וְנַקֵּה לֹא יְנַקֶּה (ve-naqqeh lo yenaqqeh) = "and acquitting, he will certainly not acquit" — infinitive absolute + negated finite verb; the doubling with negation creates absolute emphasis on the impossibility of blanket immunity. **LXX:** καθαρισμῷ οὐ καθαριεῖ ("with cleansing he will not cleanse"), preserving the doubling. Note: LXX omits "and sin" (ve-khatta'ah), listing only two categories.

**8** And Moses hastened [va-yemaher] and bowed his head [va-yiqqod] toward the ground [artsah] and bowed down [va-yishtakhu].

> **Key terms:** וַיְמַהֵר (va-yemaher) = "and he hastened" — Piel of מהר; urgency of worship. וַיִּקֹּד (va-yiqqod) = "and he bowed his head" — distinct from full prostration. **LXX:** κύψας... προσεκύνησεν ("bowing... he worshipped").

**9** And he said, "If indeed [im-na] I have found favor [khen] in your eyes, O Lord [Adonai], let the Lord [Adonai], I pray, go [yelekh-na] in our midst [be-qirbenu]; for it is a stiff-necked [qesheh-oref] people [am hu]; and you shall pardon [ve-salakhta] our iniquity [la-avonenu] and our sin [u-le-khattatenu], and you shall take us as an inheritance [u-nekhalitanu]."

> **Key terms:** וּנְחַלְתָּנוּ (u-nekhalitanu) = "and you shall take us as your inheritance/possession" — Qal perfect of נחל with suffix; Israel asks to be YHWH's own estate. Moses turns "stiff-necked" from a curse (33:3) into a plea — precisely because they are stubborn, they need YHWH in their midst. **LXX:** κληρονόμησον ἡμᾶς ("inherit us"), matching MT.

---

## The Covenant Stipulations (vv. 10–26)

**10** And he said, "Behold [hinneh], I am cutting [koret] a covenant [berit]; before all your people [neged kol-ammekha] I will do [e'eseh] wonders [nifla'ot] which have not been created [asher lo-nivru] in all the earth [be-khol-ha-arets] and in all the nations [u-ve-khol-ha-goyim]; and all the people [kol-ha-am] in whose midst [asher attah be-qirbo] you are shall see the work of YHWH [ma'aseh YHWH], for it is fearful [nora] — that which I am doing [hu asher ani oseh] with you."

> **Key terms:** נִפְלָאֹת (nifla'ot) = "wonders/extraordinary acts" — Niphal participle of פלא; the same root as Israel being "distinguished" (33:16). נוֹרָא (nora) = "fearful/awesome" — Niphal participle of ירא. **LXX:** ἔνδοξα ("glorious things"), softening the strangeness of nifla'ot.

**11** "Keep [shemor] for yourself that which I am commanding you [et-asher anokhi metsavvekha] today [ha-yom]. Behold, I am driving out [goresh] before you [mi-panekha] the Amorite, and the Canaanite, and the Hittite, and the Perizzite, and the Hivite, and the Jebusite."

> **Key terms:** גֹּרֵשׁ (goresh) = "driving out" — Qal participle; ongoing divine action. **LXX:** ἐγὼ ἐκβάλλω ("I am casting out"), matching MT.

**12** "Guard yourself [hishamer lekha] lest [pen] you cut [tikhrot] a covenant [berit] with the inhabitant [le-yoshev] of the land [ha-arets] upon which [asher] you are entering [attah ba aleha], lest it become [pen yihyeh] a snare [le-moqesh] in your midst [be-qirbekha]."

> **Key terms:** מוֹקֵשׁ (moqesh) = "snare/trap" — a hunter's device; pagan covenants become traps. **LXX:** πρόσκομμα ("stumbling-block"), a different image.

**13** "Rather [ki], their altars [et-mizbekhotam] you shall tear down [tittotsun], and their pillars [ve-et-matsevotam] you shall shatter [teshabberun], and their Asherah-poles [ve-et-asherav] you shall cut down [tikhrotun]."

> **Key terms:** מַצֵּבֹתָם (matsevotam) = "their standing-stones/pillars" — from נצב; sacred cultic pillars. אֲשֵׁרָיו (asherav) = "his/its Asherah-poles" — wooden cult objects associated with the goddess Asherah. **LXX:** τὰς στήλας ("the pillars") and τὰ ἄλση ("the groves").

**14** "For you shall not bow down [lo tishtakhaveh] to another god [le-el akher], for YHWH — Jealous [Qanna] is his name [shemo] — he is a jealous [qanna] God [El]."

> **Key terms:** קַנָּא (Qanna) = "Jealous" — used here as a divine name/title; from קנא, expressing YHWH's exclusive relational claim. שְׁמוֹ (shemo) = "his name" — Jealousy is not a flaw but a defining attribute; YHWH's identity is inseparable from his exclusive covenant demand. **LXX:** ζηλωτὸν ὄνομα ("Zealous is his name"), translating the title.

**15** "Lest [pen] you cut [tikhrot] a covenant with the inhabitant of the land, and they prostitute themselves [ve-zanu] after their gods [akharei eloheihem], and they sacrifice [ve-zavkhu] to their gods, and one calls you [ve-qara lekha] and you eat [ve-akhalta] from his sacrifice [mi-zivkho] —"

> **Key terms:** וְזָנוּ (ve-zanu) = "and they prostitute themselves" — Qal of זנה; the marital/covenantal metaphor for idolatry. **LXX:** ἐκπορνεύσωσιν ("they commit fornication"), matching MT.

**16** "— and you take [ve-laqakhta] from his daughters [mi-benotav] for your sons [le-vanekha], and his daughters prostitute themselves [ve-zanu venotav] after their gods, and they cause your sons to prostitute themselves [ve-hiznu et-banekha] after their gods."

> **Key terms:** וְהִזְנוּ (ve-hiznu) = "and they cause to prostitute" — Hiphil of זנה; the foreign wives become agents of apostasy. **LXX:** ἐκπορνεῦσαι... τοὺς υἱούς σου ("to cause your sons to fornicate").

**17** "Molten gods [elohei massekha] you shall not make [lo ta'aseh] for yourself."

> **Key terms:** מַסֵּכָה (massekha) = "molten image/casting" — from נסך ("to pour out"); a direct allusion to the golden calf of ch. 32. **LXX:** θεοὺς χωνευτούς ("cast gods"), matching MT.

**18** "The Festival of Unleavened-bread [khag ha-matsot] you shall keep [tishmor]; seven days [shiv'at yamim] you shall eat [tokhal] unleavened-bread [matsot] as I commanded you [asher tsivvitikha], at the appointed time [le-mo'ed] of the month of Abib [khodesh ha-Aviv], for in the month of Abib [be-khodesh ha-Aviv] you went out [yatsata] from Egypt [mi-Mitsrayim]."

> **Key terms:** אָבִיב (Aviv) = "fresh/young grain" — the spring month of the exodus. **LXX:** τῶν ἀζύμων ("of unleavened things"), matching MT.

**19** "Every opener [kol-peter] of a womb [rekhem] is mine [li], and all your livestock [kol-miqnekha] that is remembered as male [tizzakhar zakhar] — the opener [peter] of ox [shor] and sheep [va-seh]."

> **Key terms:** כָּל־פֶּטֶר רֶחֶם (kol-peter rekhem) = "every opener of a womb" — i.e., every firstborn; from פטר ("to open/break through"). תִּזָּכָר (tizzakhar) = "is remembered as male" — Niphal of זכר, with the noun זָכָר (zakhar, "male") creating a wordplay. **LXX:** πᾶν διανοῖγον μήτραν ("every [thing] opening a womb").

**20** "And the opener [u-feter] of a donkey [khamor] you shall redeem [tifdeh] with a lamb [be-seh]; and if you do not redeem [ve-im lo tifdeh], then you shall break its neck [va-arafto]; every firstborn [kol-bekhor] of your sons [banekha] you shall redeem [tifdeh]; and they shall not appear [ve-lo yera'u] before my face [panai] empty [reqam]."

> **Key terms:** וַעֲרַפְתּוֹ (va-arafto) = "then you shall break its neck" — Qal of ערף; the unredeemed firstborn must be killed. רֵיקָם (reqam) = "empty-handed." **LXX:** λυτρώσῃ ("you shall ransom"), matching MT.

**21** "Six days [sheshet yamim] you shall work [ta'avod], and on the seventh day [u-va-yom ha-shevi'i] you shall rest [tishbot]; in plowing time [be-kharish] and in harvest [u-va-qatsir] you shall rest [tishbot]."

> **Key terms:** בֶּחָרִישׁ וּבַקָּצִיר (be-kharish u-va-qatsir) = "in plowing and in harvest" — even at the busiest agricultural seasons, Sabbath observance is non-negotiable. **LXX:** τῷ σπόρῳ καὶ τῷ ἀμήτῳ ("in sowing and in reaping") — substitutes "sowing" for "plowing."

**22** "And the Festival of Weeks [ve-khag shavu'ot] you shall observe [ta'aseh lekha] — the firstfruits [bikkurei] of the wheat harvest [qetsir khittim] — and the Festival of Ingathering [ve-khag ha-asif] at the turn [tequfat] of the year [ha-shanah]."

> **Key terms:** שָׁבֻעֹת (shavu'ot) = "weeks" — the festival counted seven weeks from Passover. תְּקוּפַת (tequfat) = "turning/circuit" — from נקף; the autumn turning-point of the year. **LXX:** ἑορτὴν ἑβδομάδων ("festival of weeks"), matching MT.

**23** "Three times [shalosh pe'amim] in the year [ba-shanah] every male [kol-zekhurrkha] of yours shall appear [yera'eh] before the face [et-pene] of the Lord [ha-Adon], YHWH, God of Israel [Elohei Yisra'el]."

> **Key terms:** הָאָדֹן (ha-Adon) = "the Lord/Master" — the rare absolute form, used for YHWH as sovereign overlord. The full title — ha-Adon YHWH Elohei Yisra'el — is maximally emphatic. **LXX:** κυρίου τοῦ θεοῦ Ισραηλ ("of the Lord God of Israel").

**24** "For I will dispossess [ki ohrish] nations [goyim] before you [mi-panekha] and I will enlarge [ve-hirkhavti] your border [et-gevulekha]; and no man [ve-lo yakhmod ish] shall covet [yakhmod] your land [et-artsekha] when you go up [ba-alotekha] to appear [lera'ot] before the face [et-pene] of YHWH your God three times [shalosh pe'amim] in the year [ba-shanah]."

> **Key terms:** יַחְמֹד (yakhmod) = "shall covet/desire" — from חמד; YHWH guarantees territorial security during the pilgrimage festivals. אוֹרִישׁ (ohrish) = "I will dispossess/drive out" — Hiphil of ירשׁ. **LXX:** ἐκβαλῶ ἔθνη ("I will cast out nations").

**25** "You shall not slaughter [lo-tishkhat] upon leaven [al-khamets] the blood [dam] of my sacrifice [zivkhi]; and the sacrifice [ve-zevakh] of the Festival of Passover [khag ha-Pesakh] shall not remain overnight [lo yalin] until the morning [la-boqer]."

> **Key terms:** חָמֵץ (khamets) = "leaven/leavened" — Passover blood and leaven are incompatible. לֹא יָלִין (lo yalin) = "shall not spend the night" — Qal of לין; the Passover sacrifice must be consumed before dawn. **LXX:** οὐ κοιμηθήσεται ("shall not sleep"), a vivid rendering.

**26** "The first [reshit] of the firstfruits [bikkurei] of your ground [admatekha] you shall bring [tavi] to the house [beit] of YHWH your God. You shall not boil [lo-tevashel] a kid [gedi] in the milk [ba-khalev] of its mother [immo]."

> **Key terms:** רֵאשִׁית בִּכּוּרֵי (reshit bikkurei) = "the first of the firstfruits" — a double superlative. לֹא־תְבַשֵּׁל גְּדִי בַּחֲלֵב אִמּוֹ (lo-tevashel gedi ba-khalev immo) = "you shall not boil a kid in its mother's milk" — the third and final occurrence of this law (see 23:19, Deut 14:21); the basis for the rabbinic separation of meat and milk. **LXX:** οὐχ ἑψήσεις ἄρνα ἐν γάλακτι μητρὸς αὐτοῦ ("you shall not boil a lamb in its mother's milk") — renders גְּדִי as "lamb."

---

## The Writing of the Covenant and Moses' Shining Face (vv. 27–35)

**27** And YHWH said to Moses, "Write [ketov] for yourself these words [et-ha-devarim ha-elleh], for upon the mouth [al-pi] of these words [ha-devarim ha-elleh] I have cut [karati] a covenant [berit] with you [ittekha] and with Israel."

> **Key terms:** עַל־פִּי (al-pi) = "upon the mouth/according to" — the covenant rests on the precise wording of these spoken terms. **LXX:** ἐπὶ γὰρ τῶν λόγων τούτων ("for upon these words"), matching MT.

**28** And he was there with YHWH forty days [arba'im yom] and forty nights [ve-arba'im lailah]; bread [lekhem] he did not eat [lo akhal] and water [u-mayim] he did not drink [lo shatah]; and he wrote [va-yikhtov] upon the tablets [al-ha-lukhot] the words [et-divrei] of the covenant [ha-berit] — the ten words [aseret ha-devarim].

> **Key terms:** עֲשֶׂרֶת הַדְּבָרִים (aseret ha-devarim) = "the ten words" — the origin of the term "Decalogue" (Greek δεκάλογος). The subject of "he wrote" is grammatically ambiguous (Moses or YHWH); cf. v. 1 where YHWH says "I will write." **LXX:** τὰ δέκα ῥήματα ("the ten sayings"), matching MT.

**29** And it was, when Moses descended [be-redet Moshe] from Mount Sinai, and the two tablets of the testimony [shnei lukhot ha-edut] were in the hand of Moses when he descended [be-ridto] from the mountain — and Moses did not know [ve-Moshe lo yada] that the skin of his face [ki qaran or panav] shone [qaran] in his speaking [be-dabbero] with him.

> **Key terms:** קָרַן (qaran) = "shone/emitted rays" — from קֶרֶן (qeren, "horn/ray"); the verb is denominative, meaning "to send out horn-like rays." This is the sole occurrence of the verb in the Qal in the Hebrew Bible. The Vulgate famously rendered this as cornuta esset ("was horned"), producing the iconographic tradition of a horned Moses (e.g., Michelangelo). **LXX:** δεδόξασται ἡ ὄψις τοῦ χρώματος τοῦ προσώπου αὐτοῦ ("the appearance of the skin of his face was glorified") — interprets the radiance as glory, avoiding the "horn" ambiguity.

**30** And Aaron and all the Sons of Israel saw [va-yar] Moses, and behold [ve-hinneh], the skin of his face [or panav] shone [qaran], and they were afraid [va-yiru] to come near [mi-geshet] to him.

> **Key terms:** וַיִּירְאוּ (va-yiru) = "and they feared" — from ירא; the radiance of YHWH's presence on Moses' face produces the same fear as theophany itself. **LXX:** ἐφοβήθησαν ("they were afraid"), matching MT.

**31** And Moses called [va-yiqra] to them, and Aaron and all the leaders [ha-nesi'im] in the congregation [ba-edah] returned [va-yashuvu] to him, and Moses spoke [va-yedabber] to them.

> **Key terms:** הַנְּשִׂיאִם (ha-nesi'im) = "the leaders/chiefs" — from נשׂא ("to lift up"); those elevated in authority. The leaders approach first, then the people. **LXX:** οἱ ἄρχοντες ("the rulers").

**32** And afterward [ve-akharei-khen] all the Sons of Israel drew near [nigshu], and he commanded them [va-yetsavvem] all [et kol] that YHWH had spoken [dibber] with him on Mount Sinai.

> **Key terms:** נִגְּשׁוּ (nigshu) = "they drew near" — Niphal of נגשׁ; the people approach only after the leaders. **LXX:** προσήλθον ("they came near"), matching MT.

**33** And Moses finished [va-yekhal] speaking [mi-dabber] with them, and he put [va-yitten] upon his face a veil [masveh].

> **Key terms:** מַסְוֶה (masveh) = "veil/covering" — occurs only here and in vv. 34–35 in the Hebrew Bible; from an uncertain root, possibly related to סוה ("to cover"). **LXX:** κάλυμμα ("covering/veil") — the term Paul uses in 2 Corinthians 3:13–16 for his extended midrash on this passage.

**34** And when Moses entered [u-ve-vo Moshe] before the face of [lifne] YHWH to speak [le-dabber] with him, he would remove [yasir] the veil [ha-masveh] until [ad] he came out [tseto]; and he would come out [ve-yatsa] and speak [ve-dibber] to the Sons of Israel that which [et asher] he was commanded [yetsuvveh].

> **Key terms:** יָסִיר (yasir) = "he would remove" — Hiphil of סור; the veil is removed in YHWH's presence, restored before the people. **LXX:** περιῃρεῖτο τὸ κάλυμμα ("he would take off the covering").

**35** And the Sons of Israel saw [ve-ra'u] the face of Moses [et-pene Moshe], that the skin of the face of Moses [or pene Moshe] shone [qaran]; and Moses put back [ve-heshiv Moshe] the veil [et-ha-masveh] upon his face until he entered [ad-bo'o] to speak [le-dabber] with him.

> **Key terms:** וְהֵשִׁיב (ve-heshiv) = "and he returned/put back" — Hiphil of שׁוב; a repeated cycle: veil off before YHWH, veil on before the people. The chapter ends with Moses as the permanent mediator between the radiant holiness of YHWH and the fearful people — his face the living boundary between divine glory and human frailty. **LXX:** ἐπέθηκεν... τὸ κάλυμμα ("he put on... the covering"), matching MT.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **The Attribute Formula (vv. 6–7):** The proclamation רַחוּם וְחַנּוּן אֶרֶךְ אַפַּיִם וְרַב־חֶסֶד וֶאֱמֶת ("compassionate and gracious, slow of anger and abundant of loyal-love and truth") becomes the most frequently quoted self-description of YHWH in the Hebrew Bible. It is echoed in Numbers 14:18, Nehemiah 9:17, Psalms 86:15, 103:8, 145:8, Joel 2:13, Jonah 4:2, and Nahum 1:3 — always with selective adaptation. The formula centers mercy (six attributes) before judgment (one), establishing a deliberate asymmetry: khesed extends to thousands; punishment reaches only to the fourth generation.

2. **The Infinitive Absolute in v. 7:** וְנַקֵּה לֹא יְנַקֶּה ("and acquitting, he will certainly not acquit") is one of the most theologically consequential uses of the infinitive absolute + negation pattern. It does not contradict the mercy that precedes it but limits it: YHWH forgives (nose avon), yet guilt has consequences that are not simply erased. The LXX preserves the construction; the Targumim paraphrase: "acquitting those who repent but not acquitting those who do not repent."

3. **Who Wrote the Tablets? (vv. 1, 27–28):** In v. 1, YHWH says "I will write"; in v. 27, YHWH tells Moses "write for yourself these words"; in v. 28, "he wrote upon the tablets" — the subject ambiguous. The most likely resolution: YHWH wrote the Ten Words on the tablets (v. 1, 28b), while Moses wrote the covenant stipulations (vv. 11–26) in a separate document (v. 27). The LXX does not resolve the ambiguity.

4. **Qaran: Horns or Rays? (v. 29):** The verb קָרַן (qaran) is denominative from קֶרֶן (qeren), which means both "horn" and "ray of light." The context (face, speaking with God, the people's fear) decisively favors "radiated/shone." Jerome's Vulgate rendering cornuta ("horned") likely reflects awareness of the Aquila tradition. The LXX's δεδόξασται ("was glorified") is interpretive but accurate. Paul's reading in 2 Corinthians 3:7–18 builds on the LXX's δόξα ("glory") framework, turning the veil into a symbol of the old covenant's fading glory.

5. **The Veil Cycle (vv. 33–35):** The Hebrew describes a repeated pattern: Moses removes the veil before YHWH (unmediated encounter), speaks YHWH's commands to Israel unveiled (transmitting the word), then replaces the veil (concealing the residual radiance). Paul in 2 Corinthians 3:13 adds a purpose not stated in the Hebrew: that Moses veiled himself "so that the Sons of Israel would not gaze at the end of what was fading." The MT gives no such motive — it simply describes the practice.

6. **The "Ritual Decalogue" (vv. 10–26):** Scholars have sometimes called these stipulations the "Ritual Decalogue" in contrast to the "Ethical Decalogue" of ch. 20. The MT text itself does not make this distinction. The "ten words" of v. 28 most naturally refers back to the original tablets (v. 1), not to the covenant stipulations of vv. 10–26, which number more than ten.

7. **LXX Divergences:** Vaticanus follows MT closely throughout ch. 34, with minor differences: rendering גְּדִי (gedi, "kid") as ἄρνα ("lamb") in v. 26; substituting "sowing" for "plowing" in v. 21; and the interpretive δεδόξασται ("was glorified") for קָרַן ("shone/rayed") in v. 29. The most significant LXX divergence is the consistent avoidance of horn-imagery in favor of glory-language.

8. **Masoretic Division:** The Leningrad Codex shows a setumah after v. 9, marking the transition from theophany to covenant stipulations. A petuhah (open paragraph break) appears before v. 27, separating the stipulations from the writing of the covenant. Another division appears before v. 29, introducing the veil narrative as a distinct unit.
